summary: First English Poet Laureate. Restoration poet/dramatist. Known for Mac Flecknoe (satirizing Shadwell), Absalom and Achitophel, The Hind and the Panther, All for Love, Marriage a la Mode, Essay of Dramatick Poesie.

clues.clue: Opens 'All human things are subject to decay'; satirizes Shadwell as 'True-Blue Protestant Poet'
examples: satirized the 'true-blue Protestant Poet' Thomas Shadwell

clues.clue: Shadwell 'never deviates into sense'; 'confirm'd in full stupidity'
Shadwell anointed with 'mighty mug of potent ale'; falls through trapdoor
examples: anointed with 'a mighty mug of potent ale'

clues.clue: Dispute centered on different estimates of Ben Jonson's genius
examples: Shadwell fancied himself an heir to Ben Jonson

works.name: Absalom and Achitophel
works.description: Satirizes Duke of Monmouth and Earl of Shaftesbury as biblical rebels against David (Charles II).

clues.clue: Monmouth and Shaftesbury as biblical rebels against David
examples: Duke of Monmouth and Earl of Shaftesbury are satirized as biblical figures

works.name: The Hind and the Panther
works.description: Allegorical poem: Hind = Catholic Church, Panther = Anglican Church.

clues.clue: Hind = Catholic Church, Panther = Anglican; written after conversion
examples: two title animals represent the Catholic Church and the Anglican Church

clues.clue: Milk-white creature 'immortal and unchang'd'; coined 'blaze of glory'

works.name: Plays
works.description: All for Love, Marriage a la Mode, Aureng-Zebe, Conquest of Granada.

clues.clue: All for Love — adaptation of Antony and Cleopatra set in Alexandria
examples: his adaptation set entirely in Alexandria

clues.clue: Marriage a la Mode — Doralice sings 'Why should a foolish marriage vow'; set in Sicily
First Poet Laureate under Charles II; received 'butt of Canary wine'
examples: first official poet laureate under Charles II

clues.clue: Essay of Dramatick Poesie — Neander calls Shakespeare 'greatest soul'
Annus Mirabilis — Great Fire of London 1666
Parodied as Bayes in Villiers's The Rehearsal
T.S. Eliot blamed for 'dissociation of sensibility'
examples: blames Milton and this other author for the 'dissociation of sensibility'
landmark English translation of the Aeneid

comprehensive_summary: John Dryden was the first English Poet Laureate under Charles II. Mac Flecknoe satirizes Shadwell. Absalom and Achitophel targets Monmouth and Shaftesbury. The Hind and the Panther defends Catholicism allegorically. His plays include All for Love and Marriage a la Mode. He translated Virgil and wrote Essay of Dramatick Poesie.

Poem: Duke of Monmouth and Earl of Shaftesbury satirized as biblical figures rebelling against David
Poem: allegorical — Hind = Catholic Church, Panther = Anglican
